blocks:
Sūrat al-ʿAnkabūt

Meccan, and it is 69 verses

Sūrat al-ʿAnkabūt is entirely Meccan

According to one saying, it is Medinan in its latter part, except for ten verses from its beginning, which are Medinan according to a third saying. The number of its verses is sixty-nine verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الم (1) الم (Alif, Lām, Mīm.)

(٢) أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يُتْرَكُوا أَنْ يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لا يُفْتَنُونَ ** (2) أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يُتْرَكُوا أَنْ يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لا يُفْتَنُونَ (Do the people suppose that they will be let off if they say, 'We believe,' and they will not be tested?) They will not be tested (لَا يُخْتَبَرُونَ).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The meaning of يُفْتَنُونَ ** (they will be tested) is that they will be afflicted (يُبْتَلَوْنَ) in their souls (أَنْفُسِهِمْ) and their wealth (أَمْوَالِهِمْ). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When this verse was revealed, He said: "There must inevitably be a trial (فِتْنَة) by which the Ummah will be afflicted after its Prophet, so that the truthful may be distinguished from the liar, because the revelation has ceased, and what remains is the sword (السَّيْف) and the division of the word (افْتِرَاق الْكَلِمَة) until the Day of Resurrection."

In Nahj al-Balagha: A man stood up and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, inform us about the trial (الْفِتْنَة). Did you ask the Messenger of Allah about it?" Ali (peace be upon him) said: "When Allah, the Glorified, revealed الم أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ —the verse—I knew that the trial would not cease to afflict us while the Messenger of Allah was still among us. So I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what is this trial that Allah has informed you about?' He said: 'O Ali, My Ummah will certainly be tested after Me.' So I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, did You not tell me on the Day of Uhud, when those Muslims who were martyred were martyred, and martyrdom was withheld from me, which distressed me, You said to me: "Rejoice, for martyrdom is ahead of you"?' He said to me: 'That is indeed so. How will your patience be then?' I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is not a place for patience, but rather a place for good tidings (الْبُشْرَى) and gratitude (الشُّكْر).' He said: 'O Ali, they will be tested by their wealth, and they will confer favor (يَمُنُّونَ) upon their Lord with their religion, and they will wish for His mercy while feeling secure from His might (سَطْوَتَهُ). They will deem His prohibitions lawful through false ambiguities (الشُّبُهَات الْكَاذِبَة) and heedless desires (الْأَهْوَاء السَّاهِيَة). Thus, they will deem wine (الْخَمْر) lawful by calling it nabīdh (النَّبِيذ), and unlawful gain (السُّحْت) by calling it a gift (الْهَدِيَّة), and usury (الرِّبَا) by calling it a sale (الْبَيْع).' I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, then in which station (الْمَنَازِل) should I place them? In the station of apostasy (رِدَّة) or in the station of trial (فِتْنَة)?' He said: 'In the station of trial (فِتْنَة).'"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said: Al-Abbas came to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "Go, so that the people may pledge allegiance to you." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "Do you think they will do it?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Then where is the saying of Him, the Mighty and Glorious: الم أَحْسِبَ النَّاسُ —the verse?"

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He recited this verse, then said: "What is the trial (الْفِتْنَة)?" It was said: "The trial is in the religion (الدِّين)." He said: "They will be tested (يُفْتَنُونَ) just as gold is tested (يُفْتَن)." Then He said: "They will be purified (يَخْلُصُونَ) just as gold is purified (يَخْلُص)."

(٣) وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (3) And certainly We have tried those who were before them. We tested them. For that is an ancient practice (سُنَّة) running through all nations, so its opposite should not be expected. فَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللهُ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْكاذِبِينَ so Allah will surely know those who are truthful, and He will surely know the liars.** He will surely know them in existence, while they are being tested, such that those who were truthful in faith and those who lied concerning it are distinguished, after He already knew them before that—that they would come into existence and be tested.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): They read (the verbs) with a ḍammah on the yā' and a kasrah on the lām in both instances, derived from (الإعلام) (making known), meaning: so that the people may know them.

(٤) أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ ** (The meaning is) disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي) أَنْ يَسْبِقُونا ** (Meaning) that they will escape Us, so that We are unable to recompense them for their evil deeds. ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ (4) Do those who commit misdeeds suppose that they will outrun Us? Evil is the judgement that they make.

(٥) مَنْ كانَ يَرْجُوا لِقاءَ اللهِ فَإِنَّ أَجَلَ اللهِ لَآتٍ ** (5) Whoever expects to encounter Allah, [let him know that] Allah’s term is surely bound to come, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. Al-Qummi said: Whoever loves لِقاءَ اللهِ ** (the encounter with Allah), the appointed time (الأجل) will come to him. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): It means whoever believes that he will be resurrected, then the promise of Allah لَآتٍ ** (is surely bound to come), referring to reward (الثواب) and punishment (العقاب). He (peace be upon him) said: The encounter (اللّقاء) here is not by sight (بالرّؤية); rather, the encounter (اللّقاء) is the Resurrection (البعث). وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** (He is) the Hearer of the sayings of the servants الْعَلِيمُ ** (He is) the Knower of their beliefs (بعقائدهم) and their deeds (وأعمالهم).

(6) وَمَنْ جاهَدَ (6) And whoever wages struggle al-Qummi said: [He struggles against] his soul (نفسه) concerning pleasures (اللّذات), desires (الشهوات), and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). فَإِنَّما يُجاهِدُ لِنَفْسِهِ ** because its benefit is for it (the soul). إِنَّ اللهَ لَغَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعالَمِينَ ** he only struggles for his own soul. Indeed Allah is needless of the creatures. So He has no need for their obedience.

(7) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَنُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَحْسَنَ الَّذِي كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (7) And those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We will surely absolve them of their misdeeds, and We will surely pay them the best of what they used to do.** The best reward for their deeds.

(8) وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنْسانَ بِوالِدَيْهِ حُسْناً ** (8) We have enjoined man to treat his parents kindly. al-Qummi said: They are the two who gave birth to him. وَإِنْ جاهَداكَ لِتُشْرِكَ بِي ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ ** But if they urge you to ascribe to Me as partner that of which you have no knowledge, [Knowledge] of His divinity (بالهيّته). He expressed the negation of it (the partnership) by negating the knowledge of it, indicating that whatever is not known to be correct is not permissible to follow, even if its falsehood is not known, let alone what is known to be false. فَلا تُطِعْهُما ** do not obey them. In that matter, for there is no obedience to a creature in disobedience to the Creator. إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ To Me will be your return, whereat I will inform you concerning what you used to do,** [By informing you] of the recompense for it.

(٩) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَنُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ فِي الصَّالِحِينَ ** (9) Those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We shall surely admit them among the righteous. in their company. (١٠) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَقُولُ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ فَإِذا أُوذِيَ فِي اللهِ جَعَلَ فِتْنَةَ النَّاسِ كَعَذابِ اللهِ ** (10) Among the people are some who say, 'We have faith in Allah,' but when they are tormented in Allah's cause, they regard the persecution of the people as Allah's punishment. Al-Qummi said: When a person harms him, or affliction, poverty (فاقة), or fear of the oppressors (الظالمين) befalls him, he joins them in their religion, and thus he sees that what they are doing is like the punishment of Allah which does not cease. وَلَئِنْ جاءَ نَصْرٌ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (10) ...And if a victory comes from your Lord... A conquest (فتح) and spoils (غنيمة). And al-Qummi means al-Qa'im (peace be upon him). لَيَقُولُنَّ إِنَّا كُنَّا مَعَكُمْ ** (10) ...they will surely say, 'We were indeed with you.' in the religion, so include us in it. أَوَلَيْسَ اللهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِما فِي صُدُورِ الْعالَمِينَ ** (10) Is not Allah the best knower of what is in the breasts of the creatures? of sincerity (الإخلاص) and hypocrisy (النفاق).

(١١) وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا with their hearts (بقلوبهم) وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ so that He may recompense both parties (الفريقين). (11) Allah will surely ascertain those who have faith, and He will surely ascertain the hypocrites.

(١٢) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّبِعُوا سَبِيلَنا وَلْنَحْمِلْ خَطاياكُمْ. (12) The faithless say to those who have faith, "Follow our way, and let us bear your iniquities."

Al-Qummi said: The disbelievers used to say to the believers: "Be with us, for that which you fear is nothing. If it is true, we shall bear your sins." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), will punish them twice: once for their own sins and once for the sins of others.

وَما هُمْ بِحامِلِينَ مِنْ خَطاياهُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ Yet they will not bear anything of their iniquities. They are indeed liars.

(١٣) وَلَيَحْمِلُنَّ أَثْقالَهُمْ ** (13) And they will surely carry their own burdens, The burdens of what their souls committed, وَأَثْقالاً مَعَ أَثْقالِهِمْ ** and other burdens along with their own burdens. And other burdens (أَثْقالاً) along with them, because of what they caused through misguidance (الإضلال) and inciting (الحمل) to disobedience (المعصية), without anything being diminished from the burdens of those who followed them. وَلَيُسْئَلُنَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** And they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection A questioning of reproach (تقريع) and condemnation (تبكيت), عَمَّا كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** concerning what they used to fabricate. Of the falsehoods (الأباطيل) by which they misguided others.

(١٤) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً إِلى قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عاماً (14) Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them for a thousand years, less fifty years.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): No one shared in his prophethood (نبوّته). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He called them secretly and openly. So when they refused and acted insolently, he said: "My Lord, أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانْتَصِرْ I am overcome, so help [me]!" فَأَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوفانُ وَهُمْ ظالِمُونَ So the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.

(١٥) فَأَنْجَيْناهُ وَأَصْحابَ السَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْناها آيَةً لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (15) So We delivered him and the occupants of the Ark, and made it a sign for all the nations. They take heed and derive evidence from it. (١٦) وَإِبْراهِيمَ إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَاتَّقُوهُ ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ** (16) And Abraham, when he said to his people, 'Worship Allah and be wary of Him. That is better for you, should you know. than what you are upon إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (١٧) إِنَّما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَوْثاناً وَتَخْلُقُونَ إِفْكاً ** and you lie a lie (كذباً) in naming them deities (آلهة) or claiming their intercession (شفاعتها) with Allah إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ لا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ رِزْقاً فَابْتَغُوا عِنْدَ اللهِ الرِّزْقَ وَاعْبُدُوهُ وَاشْكُرُوا لَهُ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ** (17) You only worship idols instead of Allah, and you invent a lie. Indeed, those whom you worship instead of Allah do not control any provision for you. So seek provision from Allah, and worship Him, and thank Him. To Him you shall be brought back.'

(١٨) وَإِنْ تُكَذِّبُوا ** (18) And if you deny (The reading is) "And if you deny me." It is said that this is part of the story of Ibrahim (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi (said): The narrative of Ibrahim was interrupted, and Allah addressed the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, saying: وَإِنْ تُكَذِّبُوا ** (And if you deny) up to His saying لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (there is a painful punishment for them). Then He returned to the narrative of Ibrahim (peace be upon him), saying: فَما كانَ جَوابَ قَوْمِهِ ** (But the only answer of his people was). So, this is an interrupted and conjoined passage (المنقطع المعطوف).

I say: The rationale for this is that the course of the story of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) is intended to console the Messenger and relieve his distress, (This is achieved) by showing that his father, the Friend of Allah (خليل الله), was afflicted in a manner similar to what he (the Messenger) was afflicted with regarding the polytheism and denial of the people, and by likening his situation among them to the situation of Ibrahim (peace be upon him) among his people. For this reason, addressing them was placed between the two parts of his story: فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أُمَمٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ ** (Indeed, generations before you have denied) the Messengers, but their denial did not harm them; rather, it harmed themselves. Thus is their denial (of you). وَما عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ.** (and the Messenger is responsible only for a manifest delivery.)

(19) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا (Have they not regarded) وقرء بالتاء على تقدير القول And it was recited (وقرء) with the tāʾ (التاء) based on the estimation of the saying (القول): كَيْفَ يُبْدِئُ اللهُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ إِنَّ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ (how Allah originates the creation, and then brings it back? Indeed, that is easy for Allah.) since He is not in need of anything in His action.

(20) قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ (Say, 'Travel in the land,') This is an address to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم if this verse is parenthetical (معترضة) within the story of Abraham (إبراهيم), as he mentioned. And according to al-Qummi, and the narration of Allah's speech to Abraham عليه السلام, if it is part of his story: فَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ بَدَأَ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ اللهُ يُنْشِئُ النَّشْأَةَ الْآخِرَةَ (and observe how He originated the creation. Then Allah will bring forth the final creation.) And it was recited (وقرء) with the opening of the shīn (الشّين) and the lengthening (المدّ): إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (Indeed Allah has power over all things.')

(٢١) يُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَرْحَمُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُقْلَبُونَ ** (21) He punishes whomever He wishes, and has mercy on whomever He wishes, and to Him you shall be brought back. — meaning: تردّون (you shall be returned). (٢٢) وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (22) You cannot frustrate [Him] in the earth, — meaning: Your Lord from apprehending you (ادراككم). وَلا فِي السَّماءِ ** (nor in the sky,) — meaning: if you flee from His decree by hiding (التّواري) in one of the two [the earth or the sky]. وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ ** (and besides Allah you do not have any guardian or helper.) — meaning: who protects you from His affliction (بلائه).

(٢٣) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ وَلِقائِهِ ** (23) But those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and His encounter— (referring) to the Resurrection (البعث) أُولئِكَ يَئِسُوا مِنْ رَحْمَتِي ** those are the ones who have despaired of My mercy, because of their denial of the Resurrection (البعث) and the Recompense (الجزاء). وَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** and those are the ones for whom there is a painful punishment. because of their disbelief (كفرهم). (٢٤) فَما كانَ جَوابَ قَوْمِهِ ** (24) The only answer of his people the people of Abraham (peace be upon him) to him إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا اقْتُلُوهُ أَوْ حَرِّقُوهُ ** was that they said, 'Kill him or burn him!' قيل It was said that this was the saying of some of them, but since it was said among them and the rest were pleased with it, it was attributed to all of them. فَأَنْجاهُ اللهُ مِنَ النَّارِ ** But Allah delivered him from the fire. meaning: So they cast him into it, and He delivered him from it by making it cool (برداً) and safe (سلاماً) for him. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ ** There are indeed signs in that in His deliverance of him from it, there are indeed signs (لَآياتٍ), which are: His preservation of him from the harm of the fire, its extinguishing despite its magnitude in a short time, and the creation of a garden (روض) in its place. لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** for a people who have faith. because they are the ones who benefit from them.

(٢٥) وَقالَ إِنَّمَا اتَّخَذْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَوْثاناً مَوَدَّةَ بَيْنِكُمْ ** (25) He said, 'You have taken idols instead of Allah only out of mutual affection in the life of the world. It was read with idāfah (الإضافة) (genitive construction), both in the accusative (منصوبة) and the nominative (مرفوعة). فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (in the life of the world), meaning: so that you may show mutual affection among yourselves and maintain contact due to your gathering upon their worship. ثُمَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ يَكْفُرُ بَعْضُكُمْ بِبَعْضٍ. (Then on the Day of Resurrection you will disown one another.) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means some of you will disassociate (يَتَبَرَّأ) from others. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The disbelief (الكفر) in this verse is disassociation (البراءة). He says: "So some of you will disassociate (فَيَبْرَأ) from others." He said: And its parallel is in Sūrat Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him), the saying of Satan: إِنِّي كَفَرْتُ بِما أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (I disown your associating me [with Allah] previously) and the saying of Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him), the Friend of the All-beneficent (خليل الرحمن): كَفَرْنا بِكُمْ ** (We disown you), meaning: We have disassociated (تَبَرَّأْنَا) from you. وَيَلْعَنُ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضاً ** (and curse one another) meaning: mutual denial (التناكر) and cursing (التلاعن) will arise among you, or between you and the idols, like His saying: وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا. (and they will be adversaries to them.) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no group that followed an Imam (ائتمّوا بإمام) in the world except that they will come on the Day of Resurrection, where he curses them and they curse him, except for you and those who are upon a state similar to yours. And in al-Mahasin, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Are you not pleased that every group will come, where some of them curse others, except for you and those who hold your saying (مَقَالَتِكُمْ)? وَمَأْواكُمُ النَّارُ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ ** (and the Fire will be your abode, and you will have no helpers) who can save you from it.

(٢٦) فَآمَنَ لَهُ لُوطٌ ** (Then Lot believed him.) And he (Lot) was his maternal nephew (ابن خالته), as preceded in their story. وَقالَ إِنِّي مُهاجِرٌ إِلى رَبِّي ** (and said, 'Indeed, I am migrating toward my Lord.') It was said (that he meant) migrating from his people to where his Lord commanded him. Al-Qummi said: (He meant) migrating from abandoning evil deeds (السّيئات) and repenting to Allah. إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** (Indeed, He is the All-mighty,) Who protects me from my enemies. الْحَكِيمُ ** (the All-wise.) Who does not command me except with that which contains my well-being (صلاحي). In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the prophethood (نبوّته) of Abraham (peace be upon him) was in Kūthā (كُوثَى), which is a village among the villages of al-Sawād (السّواد), meaning al-Kūfah (الكوفة). He said: His first affair began there, then he migrated from it, and it was not a migration of fighting (قتال). And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنِّي ذاهِبٌ إِلى رَبِّي سَيَهْدِينِ. (Indeed, I am going toward my Lord; He will guide me.)

(٢٧) وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ إِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ** (27) We gave him Isaac and Jacob, as a son and a supererogatory gift (نافلة), when he despaired of having a child from a barren old woman. And for that reason, Ishmael (إسماعيل) was not mentioned (here). وَجَعَلْنا فِي ذُرِّيَّتِهِ النُّبُوَّةَ ** and appointed prophethood in his progeny, so the prophets multiplied among them. And the Book (الْكِتابَ) includes the four Books and the Scrolls (الصّحف). وَآتَيْناهُ أَجْرَهُ فِي الدُّنْيا and We gave him his reward in this world, by granting him a child outside of its usual time, and the pure progeny, among whom are the Seal of the Prophets (خاتم الأنبياء), the Master of the Messengers (سيّد المرسلين), and the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) (peace be upon them both), and their pure progeny (عترتهما الطّيبين), and the continuation of prophethood among them, and the affiliation of the nations (الملل) to him, and the prayers (الصلاة) and praise (الثّناء) upon him until the end of time. وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** and in the Hereafter He is indeed among the righteous. (meaning) He is certainly among the perfectly righteous (الكاملين في الصلاح).

(٢٨) وَلُوطاً إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ إِنَّكُمْ ** And it was read (وقرئ) by omitting the interrogative hamzah (همزة الاستفهام), taking it as a declarative statement (على الخبر). لَتَأْتُونَ الْفاحِشَةَ ** (you commit the indecency), meaning the deed (الفعلة) that reaches the extreme in ugliness (البالغة في القبح). ما سَبَقَكُمْ بِها مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ.

(٢٨) وَلُوطاً إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الْفاحِشَةَ ما سَبَقَكُمْ بِها مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ. (28) And Lot, when he said to his people, 'Do you commit the indecency which no one in the world has committed before you?'

(٢٩) أَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجالَ وَتَقْطَعُونَ السَّبِيلَ ** (29) Are you indeed approaching men, and obstructing the way, And you expose yourselves to travelers (لِلسَّابِلَة) with indecency (بِالْفَاحِشَة) and scandal (وَالْفَضِيحَة) until the roads were cut off (انْقَطَعَتِ الطُّرُق). وَتَأْتُونَ فِي نادِيكُمُ ** and committing the evil in your gatherings? In your crowded gatherings (فِي مَجَالِسِكُنَّ الْغَاصَّة), and the term النَّادِي (assembly/club) is not used except for a place where its people are present, [committing] the reprehensible (الْمُنْكَرَ). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Rida عليه السلام: They would break wind (يَتَضَارَطُونَ) in their gatherings without modesty (حِشْمَة) or shame (حَيَاء). And al-Qummi said: Some of them would break wind upon others. And in al-Awali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: It is the flicking of pebbles (الْخَذْف). فَما كانَ جَوابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا ائْتِنا بِعَذابِ اللهِ إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ. (30) But the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Bring us Allah's punishment, if you are one of the truthful.'

(٣٠) قالَ رَبِّ انْصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** (30) He said, 'My Lord! Help me against the corruptive people.' by introducing the indecency (الفاحشة) among those who came after them. (٣١) وَلَمَّا جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا إِبْراهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرى ** (31) When Our messengers brought Abraham the good news, with the good news of the child and the supererogatory gift (النافلة). قالُوا إِنَّا مُهْلِكُوا أَهْلِ هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ ** they said, 'We are going to destroy the people of this town. the town of Sodom (سدوم). إِنَّ أَهْلَها كانُوا ظالِمِينَ. Indeed, its people have been wrongdoers. (٣٢) قالَ إِنَّ فِيها لُوطاً قالُوا نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ فِيها لَنُنَجِّيَنَّهُ ** (32) He said, 'Lot is in it.' They said, 'We know well who is in it. We will surely deliver him And it was recited with the lightened form (بالتخفيف). وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ كانَتْ مِنَ الْغابِرِينَ ** and his family, except his wife; she will be one of those who remain behind.' those who remain in the punishment. (٣٣) وَلَمَّا أَنْ جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا لُوطاً سِيءَ بِهِمْ ** (33) When Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account, distress (المساءة) and grief (الغمّ) came upon him because of them. وَضاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعاً ** and felt helpless to protect them. and his capacity (ذرعه)—meaning his ability (طاقته)—was constrained regarding their situation and the management of their affair. وَقالُوا (And they said), when they saw in him the sign of distress (الضّجرة): لا تَخَفْ وَلا تَحْزَنْ (Do not fear and do not grieve). إِنَّا مُنَجُّوكَ (We are surely going to deliver you). And it was recited with the lightened form (بالتّخفيف). وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَكَ كانَتْ مِنَ الْغابِرِينَ.** and your family, except your wife; she will be one of those who remain behind.'

(٣٤) إِنَّا مُنْزِلُونَ ** (We are going to send down) And it was recited with al-tashdid (بالتّشديد). عَلى أَهْلِ هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ رِجْزاً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (upon the people of this town a plague from the sky) a punishment (عذاباً) from it, بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ ** (because of the iniquity they commit) due to their transgression (فِسْق). (34) (We are going to send down upon the people of this town a plague from the sky because of the iniquity they commit.)**

(٣٥) وَلَقَدْ تَرَكْنا مِنْها آيَةً بَيِّنَةً لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ ** (And We have certainly left a manifest sign of it for a people who apply reason.) It is the dwelling place of Lot (لُوط), which remained as a lesson (عبرة) for the travelers (السيّارة), as preceded in their story which was explained in Surat Hūd. (35) (And We have certainly left a manifest sign of it for a people who apply reason.)**

(٣٦) وَإِلى مَدْيَنَ أَخاهُمْ شُعَيْباً فَقالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَارْجُوا الْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ (And to Midian [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, 'O my people, worship Allah, and look forward to the Last Day,') and perform what you hope for His reward (ثواب) through it. And it was said that it [the word الرجاء] is from al-rajāʾ (الرجاء) meaning fear (الخوف). وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ. (and do not act wickedly on the earth, spreading corruption.) (36) (And to Midian [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, 'O my people, worship Allah, and look forward to the Last Day, and do not act wickedly on the earth, spreading corruption.')

(٣٧) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ ** (37) But they impugned him, so the earthquake seized them, The severe earthquake (الزلزلة الشديدة) which contained the cry (الصّيحة). فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ ** and they became corpses in their homes. Kneeling (باركين) upon the knees (الرّكب), dead (ميّتين). (٣٨) وَعاداً وَثَمُودَ ** (38) And [We destroyed] 'Ad and Thamud, Meaning: And mention them both and We destroyed them both. وَقَدْ تَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ مِنْ مَساكِنِهِمْ ** and their dwellings are manifest to you. Some of their dwellings (بعض مساكنهم) when you look at them upon your passing by them. وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ أَعْمالَهُمْ Satan made their deeds fair-seeming to them, [Namely] disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ** and barred them from the way, The straight path (السبيل السويّ) which the Messengers (الرّسل) clarified for them. وَكانُوا مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ though they were insightful. They were capable (متمكنين) of looking (النظر) and seeking insight (الاستبصار), but they did not do so.

(٣٩) وَقارُونَ وَفِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ ** (And Qarun, Pharaoh and Haman.) He presented قارُونَ ** first due to the nobility of his lineage (نسبه). وَلَقَدْ جاءَهُمْ مُوسى بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَما كانُوا سابِقِينَ ** (Certainly Moses came to them with manifest proofs, but they acted arrogantly in the land, and they were not outrunning [Our punishment].) (They were not) escaping (فائتين), rather the command (أمر) of Allah overtook them. (٤٠) فَكُلًّا أَخَذْنا بِذَنْبِهِ ** (So We seized each [of them] for his sin.) فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِ حاصِباً ** (Among them were those on whom We sent a rain of stones,) (meaning) gravel (حصباء), like the people of Lot. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ أَخَذَتْهُ الصَّيْحَةُ (and among them were those who were seized by the Cry,) like the people of Midian and Thamud. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ خَسَفْنا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ ** (and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow,) like Qarun. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ أَغْرَقْنا ** (and among them were those whom We drowned.) like Pharaoh and his people, and the people of Noah. وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ ** (Allah would not wrong them,) by punishing them without a crime (جرم). وَلَكِنْ كَانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (but they used to wrong themselves.) by exposing themselves to the punishment (العذاب).

(٤١) مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَوْلِياءَ ** (41) The parable of those who take guardians besides Allah regarding what they have taken as a reliance and a dependence, كَمَثَلِ الْعَنْكَبُوتِ اتَّخَذَتْ بَيْتاً regarding what it weaves in weakness (الوهن) and fragility (الخور). وَإِنَّ أَوْهَنَ الْبُيُوتِ لَبَيْتُ الْعَنْكَبُوتِ ** is that of the spider which takes a home. Indeed the frailest of houses is the house of the spider. There is no house weaker or less protective against heat and cold than it. لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ ** Had they known! If they were to return to knowledge (علم), they would know that this is their parable.

(٤٢) إِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما And it was read with the yā’ (يَدْعُونَ) مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. (42) Indeed Allah knows whatever you invoke besides Him of anything, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٤٣) وَتِلْكَ الْأَمْثالُ meaning this parable and its likes نَضْرِبُها لِلنَّاسِ as an approximation for what is distant from their understandings وَما يَعْقِلُها إِلَّا الْعالِمُونَ ** those who reflect upon things as is proper. (43) These are the parables which We draw for mankind, and none understands them except the knowledgeable. Al-Qummi said: It means the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He recited this Ayah and then said: "The knowledgeable one (العالم) is he who understands from Allah, so he acts in obedience to Him and avoids His wrath (سخطه)."

(٤٤) خَلَقَ اللهُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (44) Allah created the heavens and the earth with the truth. There is indeed a sign in that for the faithful. Because they are the ones who benefit from it. (٤٥) اتْلُ ما أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (45) Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, seeking nearness to Allah by reciting it, memorizing its words, and exploring its meanings وَأَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ تَنْهى عَنِ الْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ and maintain the prayer. Indeed the prayer prevents indecency and wrongdoing. Al-Qummi said: "Whoever's prayer does not restrain him from indecency (الفحشاء) and wrongdoing (المنكر), it only increases his distance from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ)." In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: similar to this. And it is narrated: A young man from the Ansar used to perform the prayers with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, yet he committed indecent acts (الفواحش). This was described to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He said: "Indeed, his prayer will restrain him one day." It was not long before he repented. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, He said: "Prayer is the restraint (حجزة) of Allah, and that is because it restrains the worshipper from sins (المعاصي) as long as he is in his prayer." Then He recited this Ayah. In al-Kafi, narrated from Sa'd al-Khaffaf, from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He (Sa'd) asked Him: "Does the Quran speak?" He smiled, then said: "May Allah have mercy upon the weak among our followers (شيعتنا); they are people of submission (أهل تسليم)." Then He said: "Yes, O Sa'd, and the prayer speaks, and it has a form (صورة) and a creation (خلق); it commands and forbids." Sa'd said: "My color changed because of that, and I said: 'This is something I cannot speak about among the people.'" He عليه السلام said: "Are the people anyone other than our followers (شيعتنا)? Whoever does not know the prayer has denied our right (حقّنا)." Then He said: "O Sa'd, shall I let you hear the speech of the Quran?" Sa'd said: "Yes, may Allah bless you." So He said: إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ تَنْهى عَنِ الْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ وَلَذِكْرُ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ ** (Indeed the prayer prevents indecency and wrongdoing, and the remembrance of Allah is greater.) "So the forbidding (النّهي) is speech, and الْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** (indecency and wrongdoing) are men (رجال), and We are the remembrance of Allah (ذكر الله), and We are greater (أكبر)." I say: And الْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** (indecency and wrongdoing) are the first two (mentioned), since they are their form (صورتهما) and their creation (خلقهما), and the prayer is the one who forbids them, and He is known وَلَذِكْرُ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ. (and the remembrance of Allah is greater.) Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He says: لَذِكْرُ اللهِ ** (And the remembrance of Allah) for the people of prayer أَكْبَرُ ** (is greater) than their remembrance of Him. Do you not see that He says: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ. (So remember Me and I will remember you.) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding the saying of Allah the Almighty: وَلَذِكْرُ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ ** (and the remembrance of Allah is greater), He said: "It is the remembrance of Allah concerning what He has made lawful (أحل) and what He has made unlawful (حرّم)." وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما تَصْنَعُونَ. (and Allah knows what you do.)

(46) **وَلا تُجادِلُوا أَهْلَ الْكِتابِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** (46) Do not dispute with the People of the Book unless it is in the best manner,

Its exegesis has already passed in Sūrat al-Naḥl (The Bee) at the Almighty's saying: **وَجادِلْهُمْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ ** And debate with them in the best manner, except those of them who are wrongdoers.

—by excessiveness and transgression— **وَقُولُوا آمَنَّا بِالَّذِي أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْنا وَأُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ ** and say, 'We believe in what has been sent down to us and what has been sent down to you.

This is part of disputing in the best manner.

And it is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that he said: "Do not affirm the People of the Book nor deny them, and say: 'We believe in Allah and in His Books and His Messengers.' For if they say falsehood, do not affirm them, and if they say truth, do not deny them."

**وَإِلهُنا وَإِلهُكُمْ واحِدٌ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ ** Our God and your God is one, and to Him do we submit.'

—We are obedient to Him exclusively. And perhaps there is an allusion in it to their taking their rabbis (أحبارهم) and monks (رهبانهم) as lords besides Allah.

(٤٧) وَكَذلِكَ أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ فَالَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ. (Thus have We sent down the Book to you. Those whom We have given the Book believe in it.) Al-Qummi said: They are the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family). وَمِنْ هؤُلاءِ ** (And among these), he said: He means the people of faith (أهل الإيمان) from the people of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة), مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ وَما يَجْحَدُ بِآياتِنا ** (some believe in it, and none impugn Our signs) despite their clarity (ظهورها) and the establishment of the proof (الحجّة) concerning them, إِلَّا الْكافِرُونَ (except the faithless). Al-Qummi said: He means that none impugn the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (peace be upon them) إِلَّا الْكافِرُونَ. (except the faithless).

(٤٨) **وَما كُنْتَ تَتْلُوا مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مِنْ كِتابٍ وَلا تَخُطُّهُ بِيَمِينِكَ إِذاً لَارْتابَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ ** (48) You did not use to recite any scripture before it, nor did you write it with your right hand, for then the impugners would have been skeptical.

For the manifestation of this Book, which encompasses various types of noble sciences, upon an unlettered man (أُمّي) who was not known for reading and learning, is a supernatural occurrence (خارق للعادة). And the mention of the right hand is an added depiction of the negated action, and a negation of figurative usage (التجوّز) in the attribution (الاسناد). **إِذاً لَارْتابَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ ** (for then the impugners would have been skeptical)—meaning, if you were someone who wrote and read, they would have said: perhaps he learned it or picked it up from the books of the ancients.

Al-Qummi said: This Ayah is connected (معطوفة) to His saying in Sūrat al-Furqān: اكْتَتَبَها فَهِيَ تُمْلى عَلَيْهِ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً ** (He has had them written down, and they are dictated to him morning and evening). So Allah refuted them and said: How can they claim that what you recite or inform about, you write it from someone else, while you ما كُنْتَ تَتْلُوا مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مِنْ كِتابٍ وَلا تَخُطُّهُ بِيَمِينِكَ إِذاً لَارْتابَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ ** (You did not use to recite any scripture before it, nor did you write it with your right hand, for then the impugners would have been skeptical)—meaning, they would have doubted (شكّوا).

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a narrative (حديث): And among His signs is that he was an orphan, poor, a hired shepherd, who did not learn a book and did not frequent a teacher. Then he came with the Quran, which contains the stories of the prophets and their news, letter by letter, and the news of those who passed away and those who remain until the Day of Resurrection.

(49) بَلْ هُوَ آيَاتٌ بَيِّنَاتٌ فِي صُدُورِ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ (49) Rather, they are manifest signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge.

The meaning of **بَلْ هُوَ ** is the Quran.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah, then He pointed with His hand to His chest. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He recited it and said: "What is between the two covers of the Mushaf (المصحف)." It was said: "Who are they?" He said: "Who else could they possibly be but us?"

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them). And He said: "We are the ones, and He meant us in many narrations."

وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَّا الظَّالِمُونَ (and none rejects Our signs except the wrongdoers.)

(٥٠) وَقالُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آياتٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (50) And they say, 'Why have not signs been sent down to him from his Lord?' (like the she-camel of Salih, the staff of Musa (peace be upon him), and the table (مائدة) of Isa (peace be upon him)). And it was read (وقرئ) [as] آيات (signs) [in the plural]. قُلْ إِنَّمَا الْآياتُ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** Say, 'The signs are only with Allah.' He sends them down as He wills. I do not possess them so that I may bring you what you propose. وَإِنَّما أَنَا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ ** and I am only a manifest warner.' My concern (شأني) is nothing but the warning (الإنذار) through the signs (الآيات) I have been given.

(٥١) أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِهِمْ A sign (آية) that suffices them from what they proposed أَنَّا أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ يُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ Its recitation upon them is continuous إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ meaning, in that Book, which is a continuous sign (آية مستمرّة) and a manifest proof (حجّة مبيّنة) لَرَحْمَةً a great favor (نعمة عظيمة) وَذِكْرى لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ and an admonition (تذكرة) for those whose concern (همّه) is faith (الإيمان), not obstinacy (التعنّت).

أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِهِمْ أَنَّا أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ يُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَرَحْمَةً وَذِكْرى لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (Is it not enough for them that We have sent down to you the Book which is recited to them? There is indeed a mercy in that, and a reminder for a people who have faith.)

It is narrated: that some people among the Muslims came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله with a shoulder blade (كتف) upon which was written some of what the Jews say. So He said: "It is sufficient misguidance (ضلالة) for a people that they should prefer what their Prophet brought over what someone other than their Prophet brought." So [the Ayah] was revealed.

(٥٢) قُلْ كَفى بِاللهِ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ شَهِيداً ** (52) Say, 'Allah suffices as a witness between me and you.' regarding my truthfulness, and He has confirmed me with miracles (المعجزات). يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Thus, my condition and your condition are not hidden from Him. وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِالْباطِلِ which is what they worship besides Allah, وَكَفَرُوا بِاللهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** and disbelieve in Allah—it is they who are the losers. in their transaction, since they purchased disbelief (الكفر) in exchange for faith (الإيمان).

(53) وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذابِ ** (They ask you to hasten the punishment) by their saying فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ وَلَوْ لا أَجَلٌ (‘Rain down upon us stones from the sky.’ But were it not for a specified term) مُسَمًّى ** (specified) for every punishment and people لَجاءَهُمُ الْعَذابُ ** (the punishment would have surely overtaken them) immediately وَلَيَأْتِيَنَّهُمْ بَغْتَةً ** (and it will surely overtake them suddenly) suddenly in the world, like the Battle of Badr (وقعة بدر), or in the Hereafter when death descends upon them **وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** (while they are unaware) of its coming.

(54) يَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذابِ وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمُحِيطَةٌ بِالْكافِرِينَ ** (54) They ask you to hasten the punishment, while indeed hell encompasses the faithless. Due to the encompassing nature of its causes (أسبابها) around them. (55) يَوْمَ يَغْشاهُمُ الْعَذابُ مِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِمْ وَيَقُولُ ** (55) The day the punishment covers them from above them and from under their feet, and He will say: And it was recited with the nūn (بالنون) ذُوقُوا ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ. (Taste what you used to do.) (56) يا عِبادِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ أَرْضِي واسِعَةٌ فَإِيَّايَ فَاعْبُدُونِ ** (56) O My servants who have faith! My earth is indeed vast; so worship Me alone. Meaning: If worship is not made easy for you in one town (بلدة), then migrate to where that becomes possible for you. In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: He says: Do not obey the people of transgression (أهل الفسق) among the kings, for if you fear that they may tempt you away from your religion, then indeed أَرْضِي واسِعَةٌ ** (My earth is vast). And He says: فِيمَ كُنْتُمْ قالُوا كُنَّا مُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ قالُوا أَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَرْضُ اللهِ واسِعَةً فَتُهاجِرُوا فِيها. (What was your state? They said: 'We were oppressed in the land.' They said: 'Was not Allah’s earth vast enough for you to migrate in it?') And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If Allah is disobeyed in a land where you are, then leave it for another. And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Whoever flees with his religion from one land to another, even if it is a span (شبراً) of the earth, he deserves Paradise thereby and will be the companion (رفيق) of Abraham (peace be upon him) and Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace).

(57) كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ** (Every soul shall taste death) it will certainly attain it. ثُمَّ إِلَيْنا تُرْجَعُونَ ** (then to Us you will be brought back) And it was read with the yā’ (يُرْجَعُونَ). Narrations concerning this verse have already passed in Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān.

(58) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُمْ ** (But those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We will surely settle them) We will surely cause them to descend/lodge them. And it was read لَنُثْوِيَنَّهُمْ with the thā’ (ث), derived from الثواء (al-thawā’), meaning: We will surely establish them/make them reside, مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ غُرَفاً عوالي (from the Garden, lofty chambers). تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها نِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعامِلِينَ.** (with streams running in their vicinity, abiding in them forever. How excellent is the reward of the workers!)

(٥٩) الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا concerning trials (المحن) and hardships (المشاق) وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ and they do not put their trust except in Allah.

(٥٩) الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَعَلَى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (59) those who are patient, and in their Lord put their trust.

(٦٠) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ دَابَّةٍ لا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا اللهُ يَرْزُقُها وَإِيَّاكُمْ. (60) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ دَابَّةٍ لَا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ (60) How many a creature there is that does not carry its provision! Allah provides for it and for you.

Al-Qummi said: The Arabs used to kill their children for fear of hunger, so Allah said: اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ (Allah provides for it and for you). And it was said (وقيل): When they were commanded to emigrate (الهجرة), some of them said, "How can we enter a town where we have no means of livelihood (معيشة)?" So, it was revealed.

In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Umar, he said: We went out with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to some of the gardens (حيطان) of the Ansar. He began eating dates and said, "This is the fourth morning since I tasted food. If I wished, I would call upon my Lord, and He would give me the like of what Khosrow (كسرى) and Caesar (قيصر) possess. So how will it be for you, O son of Umar, when you remain with a people who hoard the provision of their year due to weakness of certainty (ضعف اليقين)?" By Allah, we had not departed until this verse was revealed: وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ concerning your saying and your inner thoughts (بضميركم).

وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

(61) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ فَأَنَّى يُؤْفَكُونَ (61) If you ask them, 'Who created the heavens and the earth, and subjected the sun and the moon?' they will surely say, 'Allah.' So where are they being led away? They are turned away (يُصْرَفُونَ) from His Oneness (تَوْحِيدِهِ) after their affirmation of that by innate disposition (بِالْفِطْرَة).

(62) اللهُ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ إِنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (62) Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it for him. Indeed Allah is all-knowing of all things. [The pronoun لَهُ (for him) refers] to the one for whom He expands [provision] (لِمَنْ يَبْسُطُ) in succession (عَلَى التَّعَاقُب), or to whomever He wills (لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ) due to its ambiguity (لِإِبْهَامِهِ). He knows their benefits (مَصَالِحَهُمْ) and their harms (مَفَاسِدَهُمْ).

(63) وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ نَزَّلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَحْيا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَوْتِها لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ قُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ (63) If you ask them, 'Who sends down water from the sky, and revives the earth with it after its death?' they will surely say, 'Allah.' Say, 'All praise belongs to Allah.' But most of them do not apply reason. Thus, they contradict themselves (فَيَتَنَاقَضُونَ) since they affirm that He is the Creator of everything (خَالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ), yet they associate idols (الْأَصْنَام) as partners with Him.

(٦٤) وَما هذِهِ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا إِلَّا لَهْوٌ وَلَعِبٌ ** (64) This worldly life is nothing but diversion and play. [It is] only like that with which children divert themselves and play, gathering around it and rejoicing in it for an hour, then dispersing, exhausted. وَإِنَّ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ لَهِيَ الْحَيَوانُ ** But the abode of the Hereafter is indeed the [real] life. It is indeed the abode of the true life (الحياة الحقيقيّة), due to the impossibility of death ever afflicting it. And in the word al-Hayawan (الحيوان) there is an emphasis (مبالغة) that is not present in the word al-Hayat (الحياة), because the pattern fa'alān (فَعَلَان) is built upon the movement (الحركة) and agitation (الاضطراب) necessary for life. لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ ** If they but knew! They would not prefer over it this world, whose life is transient (عارضة) and swiftly vanishing (سريعة الزّوال).

فَإِذا رَكِبُوا فِي الْفُلْكِ ** (So when they board the ships) (even) upon what they are upon of polytheism (الشرك) دَعَوُا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (they invoke Allah, dedicating their faith to Him) being in the state of those believers who dedicate their faith sincerely (أخلص دينه), since they do not mention anyone except Allah, nor do they call upon anyone besides Him, due to their knowledge that none removes hardships except Him فَلَمَّا نَجَّاهُمْ إِلَى الْبَرِّ إِذا هُمْ (١) يُشْرِكُونَ ** (But when He delivers them to the land, behold, they ascribe partners [to Him].) they immediately reverted to polytheism (الشرك).

(66) لِيَكْفُرُوا بِما آتَيْناهُمْ ** (So that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them) So that they may be ungrateful (كافرين) through their polytheism (بشركهم) for the blessing of salvation (نعمة النجاة). وَلِيَتَمَتَّعُوا (And so that they may enjoy) their gathering upon the worship of idols (عبادة الأصنام) and their mutual affection (توادّهم) for it. And it was read (وقرء) with the sukun on the lām (اللّام). فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (Then soon they will know) the consequence (عاقبة) of that when they are punished (حين يعاقبون).

(٦٧) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا ** (67) Have they not seen meaning the people of Mecca أَنَّا جَعَلْنا حَرَماً آمِناً ** that We have made a secure sanctuary, meaning, We made their land protected (مصوناً) from plunder (النهب) and aggression (التعدّي), its people safe (آمناً اهله) from killing (القتل) and captivity (السّبي) وَيُتَخَطَّفُ النَّاسُ مِنْ حَوْلِهِمْ ** while the people around them are being snatched away? They are snatched away (يختلسون) by killing and captivity, since the Arabs around it were engaged in mutual raiding (تغاور) and plundering (تناهب) أَفَبِالْباطِلِ ** Do they believe in falsehood after this manifest blessing and others which none can effect except Allah, (meaning) by the idol (بالصنم) or the devil (الشيطان) **يُؤْمِنُونَ وَبِنِعْمَةِ اللهِ يَكْفُرُونَ ** and deny the blessing of Allah? where they associate others with Him.

(68) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (68) And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah by claiming that He has a partner, أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جاءَهُ ** or denies the truth when it comes to him? when it came to him without reflection (تأمّل) or pause (توقّف). أَلَيْسَ فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوىً لِلْكافِرِينَ Is there not an abode in hell for the faithless?

(٦٩) وَالَّذِينَ جاهَدُوا فِينا ** (But those who strive in Our way) in Our right (في حقّنا), encompassing the striving (جهاد) against apparent (الظاهرة) and hidden (الباطنة) enemies. لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنا ** (We shall surely guide them to Our ways) the ways of journeying toward Us and reaching Our presence (جنابنا). And in the Hadith: "Whoever acts upon what he knows, Allah will bequeath him the knowledge of what he did not know." وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَمَعَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (and Allah is indeed with the virtuous.) with victory (بالنصر) and assistance (والإعانة).

Al-Qummi (narrated) regarding جاهَدُوا فِينا ** (strive in Our way): meaning, they were patient (صبروا) and strove (وجاهدوا) alongside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنا ** (We shall surely guide them to Our ways): meaning, We shall surely make them firm (لنثبتنّهم).

And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "This verse is for the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them) and their followers (أشياعهم)."

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from him عليه السلام, from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, who said: "Behold, I am specified in the Quran by names (بأسماء). Beware lest you be overcome regarding them, and thus go astray in your religion. I am al-Muhsin (المُحسن - the virtuous one). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: إِنَّ اللهَ لَمَعَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (and Allah is indeed with the virtuous)."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Whoever recites Surat al-Ankabut and al-Rum in the month of Ramadan on the night of the twenty-third, then by Allah, he is one of the people of Paradise. I never make an exception concerning this, nor do I fear that Allah will record a sin against me in my oath (في يميني). And indeed, these two Surahs hold a high station (لمكاناً) with Allah."

footnote (١) Meaning, when He delivered them to the land and they were safe from destruction, they returned to what they were upon: associating partners with Him in worship.
